title: 10 best herbal teas for nail fungus
intro_desc: Herbal teas for nail fungus are infusions made from plants that have antifungal properties, which can help to treat and prevent the recurrence of nail fungal infections. Examples of herbal teas that can be used include those made from calendula, lavender, and oregano. These teas can be consumed internally to boost the immune system and promote overall health, as well as used topically by soaking the affected nails in the tea. By using herbal teas, individuals can treat nail fungus in a natural and holistic way, improving their quality of life by avoiding the potential side effects of conventional antifungal treatments. Additionally, herbal teas can be a cost-effective and accessible option for those looking for a more natural approach to treating nail fungus.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Narrow-leaved paperbark teas helps with nail fungus because it contains antimicrobial compounds that effectively combat fungal infections. The tea's active ingredients, such as terpenes and phenolic acids, penetrate deep into the nail bed to destroy the underlying fungus, promoting healthy growth and clearing up discoloration and brittleness. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling associated with nail fungal infections, leading to a more natural-looking and healthy appearance of the nails.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Narrow-leaved paperbark tea contains compounds that have antimicrobial activity, which helps to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail fungus, preventing further infection.

Anti-inflammatory property: The tea also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and redness associated with nail fungal infections, promoting a healthy environment for nail growth.

Antioxidant property: Narrow-leaved paperbark tea is rich in antioxidants that help combat oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of nail fungus. Antioxidants also promote overall skin and nail health, supporting the recovery process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain associated with nail fungus.
Bark: Containing antibacterial and antifungal properties, which inhibit the growth of fungal infections on the nails.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality leaves from melaleuca alternifolia plants in the early morning or late afternoon.
Rinse the harvested leaves gently with clean water to remove any dirt or debris, then pat them dry.
Use one teaspoon of dried leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves for every 8 oz cup of boiling water, allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Enjoy your herbal narrow-leaved paperbark tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, and serve immediately after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with nail fungus because it contains antimicrobial compounds that can penetrate the nail plate and target fungal infections. The essential oils in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy nail growth. Drinking thyme tea regularly may also help to boost the immune system, which can further aid in the body's natural defense against fungal infections.
remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Thymol, a chemical compound found in thyme, has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens, including those that cause nail fungus.
Antioxidant activity: Thyme tea's high antioxidant content helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation around the affected nails, creating an environment less conducive to fungal growth.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in thyme tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe redness, itching, and swelling associated with nail fungus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain a high concentration of antifungal compounds that help combat fungal infections causing nail fungus.
Leaves: The leaves of the thyme plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healthy nail growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried thyme leaves from the thymus vulgaris plant.
Rinse the harvested thyme leaves with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one tablespoon of fresh thyme leaves or one teaspoon of dried thyme leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the thyme mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors and medicinal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids before serving hot or cold.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano teas helps with nail fungus because its antimicrobial properties effectively combat fungal infections that cause discoloration, brittleness, and thickening of nails. The tea's active compounds, such as carvacrol and thymol, penetrate the nail bed to target the underlying fungal infection, promoting healthy nail growth and resolving symptoms like itchiness, redness, and pain. Additionally, oregano tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with nail fungus.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial compounds: Oregano tea contains antimicrobial compounds like carvacrol and thymol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause nail fungus, helping to treat infections and prevent recurrences.

Antifungal properties: The antifungal properties of oregano tea help to combat fungal infections by inhibiting the production of hyphae, the branching filaments that allow fungi to spread and thrive, thereby promoting healthy nail growth.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Oregano tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and redness associated with nail fungus infections, allowing for a more comfortable and effective treatment process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Oregano roots contain compounds that have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making them effective in combating fungal infections like nail fungus.
Seeds: Oregano seeds are a good source of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help soothe and calm the skin around infected nails.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried origanum vulgare flowers for every eight ounces water.
Combine the measured oregano with boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or cup.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes allowing the flavors and oils to infuse fully.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or piece of cheesecloth if necessary.
Serve the herbal oregano tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum teas helps with nail fungus because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat the fungal infections that cause nail discoloration and brittleness. The tea's active compounds, such as eucalyptol and terpinen-4-ol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, reducing the appearance of yellowing or thickening nails and promoting healthy nail growth.
remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Tasmanian Blue Gum tea contains antifungal compounds such as terpinen-4-ol, which help to inhibit the growth and spread of fungal infections like nail fungus, reducing symptoms like discoloration and brittleness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and redness associated with nail fungus, promoting a healthy environment for nail growth and reducing discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: Tasmanian Blue Gum tea's antioxidant properties can help protect the nails from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of fungal infections and slow down nail recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of Tasmanian blue gum have been traditionally used to treat fungal infections, making them an effective choice for nail fungus tea.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Tasmanian blue gum contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat fungal growth and promote healthy nails.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves by pinching off tender shoots from mature trees in late morning or early afternoon.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours or air drying for 24 hours until brittle.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 8 ounces boiling water in a large glass teapot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes before straining it using a fine-mesh sieve and discarding solids.
Store the infused tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with nail fungus because it possesses antifungal properties that can effectively combat fungal infections causing toenail or fingernail fungus. The tea's active compounds, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, reducing the severity of symptoms like discoloration, brittleness, and cracking. By drinking rosemary tea regularly, individuals can promote a healthy nail environment that is less conducive to fungal growth, ultimately leading to clearer and stronger nails.
remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Rosemary tea contains antifungal compounds like camphor, borneol, and bornyl acetate, which help to inhibit the growth of fungal organisms that cause nail fungus.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and redness associated with nail fungus, promoting a healthy environment for nail growth.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in rosemary tea, such as carnosic acid, help protect the nails from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to fungal infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots are valued for their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating fungal infections like nail fungus.
Leaves: The leaves of rosemary contain camphor, a compound that has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria, making it helpful in combating nail fungus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosemary leaves from your garden or store-dried rosemary herbs for optimal flavor and aroma.
Rinse the dried rosemary leaves with filtered water to remove any impurities or debris.
Combine 1-2 teaspoons of dried rosemary leaves with boiling water in a teapot or infuser for steeping.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes depending on desired strength and flavor intensity.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth and enjoy warm or chilled as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic teas helps with nail fungus because it has potent antifungal properties that target the fungal infection at its root. The sulfur compounds present in garlic, such as allicin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including those that cause nail fungus. When consumed as a tea, these compounds are absorbed into the bloodstream and can reach the affected nails, promoting a healthy environment for nail growth and helping to clear up fungal infections.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Herbal garlic tea's antimicrobial property helps to combat fungal infections such as nail fungus, by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells and preventing them from spreading.

Anti-inflammatory property: Garlic tea's anti-inflammatory property reduces inflammation around the affected area, which can help to alleviate symptoms such as redness, swelling, and pain associated with nail fungus.

Antioxidant property: Herbal garlic tea's antioxidant property helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage healthy tissues, preventing further damage and promoting a healthy environment for the growth of new, fungus-free nails.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sulfur compounds, which have antifungal and antibacterial properties, making them effective against nail fungus.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of allicin, a natural compound with antimicrobial properties that can help combat fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh allium sativum bulbs by gently digging around their bases for optimal flavor.
Chop the cleaned and peeled garlic cloves into small pieces weighing approximately one-quarter teaspoon per cup of water.
Combine the chopped garlic with dried calendula petals in a heat-resistant glass teapot or infuser at a ratio of one to three parts garlic to calendula.
Pour boiling water over the mixture, allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes while releasing its pungent aroma and medicinal properties.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding solids and serving immediately or storing in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric teas helps with nail fungus because it contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which has potent antifungal properties. When consumed as a tea, curcumin is absorbed into the bloodstream and travels to the affected nails, where it works to inhibit the growth of fungal cells. This can help to gradually clear up yellowing or discoloration, promote healthy nail growth, and even prevent recurrence of the infection.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin, reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area, promoting healthy nail growth and combating fungal infections.
Antifungal properties: The antimicrobial agents present in turmeric, like curcuminoids and volatile oils, directly target and destroy fungal spores, preventing their spread and growth on the nails.

Wound-healing and antiseptic properties: Turmeric's antibacterial and antifungal compounds accelerate wound healing, reducing the risk of secondary infections and promoting a healthy environment for nail regeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant contain a high concentration of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce inflammation and combat fungal infections.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of turmeric have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various skin conditions, including nail fungus, due to their antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Boil fresh water in a pot and add one teaspoon of dried turmeric root powder or two-inch piece of fresh turmeric root.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 5-7 minutes allowing the flavors to meld together.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth removing any loose particles.
Add one tablespoon of honey or natural sweetener to taste balancing out the bitterness of the turmeric.
Enjoy your warm and soothing turmeric tea immediately as is or with milk and a squeeze of fresh lemon juice if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with nail fungus because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has antifungal properties. This compound targets the fungal infection that causes nail fungus, preventing it from spreading and promoting healthy nail growth. Additionally, licorice tea's antibacterial properties help to reduce inflammation and promote a healthy environment for new nails to grow, making it an effective natural remedy for treating nail fungus.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of fungi that cause nail fungus.

Licoricidin: This compound has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against a range of fungal and bacterial strains, including those that cause nail fungus, helping to combat infection and promote healing.

Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, creating an environment that is less conducive to fungal growth and more supportive of healthy nail growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help treat fungal infections such as nail fungus.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains triterpenoids, which have antifungal and antimicrobial properties that can help combat fungal infections and promote healthy nail growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried licorice roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in the morning after dew has dried.
Clean and cut the licorice roots into small pieces weighing 10-15 grams per serving.
Combine the chopped licorice roots with boiling water in a tea infuser or large mug holding at least 250ml.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the flavors have infused fully and the liquid has reached desired strength.
Strain the tea into a cup and serve hot or chilled, sweetening to taste if preferred with honey or sugar.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with nail fungus because of its natural antifungal properties, which can help to combat the infection and promote healthy nail growth. The antimicrobial compounds found in sage have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail infections, reducing inflammation and discomfort. By drinking sage tea regularly, individuals may be able to improve the appearance and overall health of their nails.
remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Sage tea contains antifungal compounds such as thymol, carvacrol, and borneol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal organisms that cause nail fungus.
Anti-inflammatory agents: Sage tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with nail fungus infections.

Antioxidant properties: Sage tea is rich in antioxidants like polyphenols and phenolic acids, which can help protect the nails from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy nail growth and reducing the risk of fungal infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that can help reduce inflammation and combat fungal infections in the nails.
Bark: The bark of the sage plant contains tannins, which can help dry out and heal infected areas, reducing the appearance of unsightly nail fungus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves from your garden or store-bought supply.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried sage leaves or two tablespoons of fresh sage leaves per cup.
Bring filtered water to a boil in a teapot or kettle then reduce heat to simmer.
Steep the sage leaves in hot water for 5-7 minutes allowing flavors and aromas to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or piece of cheesecloth to remove solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with nail fungus because it contains a compound called anethole, which has potent antifungal properties. This natural ingredient can help to reduce the growth of fungal spores and prevent them from spreading to other parts of the body. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of fennel tea also promote healthy circulation and immune function, allowing the nail bed to heal and recover more quickly. As a result, fennel tea is often recommended as a natural treatment for nail fungus.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Fennel tea has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal infections, including those that cause nail fungus, by reducing the ability of fungi to adhere to and penetrate the nail.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in fennel tea help protect the nails from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of nail fungus, and also reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that may exacerbate the condition.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling, redness, and discomfort associated with nail fungus, allowing for faster healing and reducing the risk of further infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help combat fungal infections and promote healthy nail growth.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain antimicrobial properties that aid in reducing nail fungus symptoms and preventing reinfection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh fennel leaves or dried fennel herb according to your recipe requirements.
Measure out one teaspoon of crushed fennel seeds or three teaspoons of dried fennel leaves per cup of boiling water.
Bring the measured water to a boil and then reduce heat to simmer for five to seven minutes.
Steep the fennel in hot water for ten to fifteen minutes, allowing its flavors to infuse and release their medicinal properties.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding solids and enjoying immediately or refrigerating for later use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat nail fungus with herbal teas is by soaking the affected nails in a tea bath. Use potent antifungal herbs such as calendula, oregano, and tea tree. Steep 1-2 tablespoons of the dried herb in boiling water for 15-20 minutes. Let the tea cool down to a warm temperature, then soak the nails for 15-20 minutes, twice a day. Consistency is key for successful treatment; continue this routine for several months to ensure the fungus is completely eliminated.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nail fungus that are treated with herbal teas include certain skin and nail infections. For instance, green tea has been found to have antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for conditions like athlete's foot or ringworm. Chamomile tea is another option, known for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help soothe skin irritations and infections. Additionally, research suggests that certain herbal teas, like those made from calendula or echinacea, may possess antifungal properties that could aid in treating nail fungus or other fungal infections. However, it's essential to consult a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment regimen.
